title: Update on the DeFi AI trading bot I tested, now on mainnet
author: emlanis
selftext:
I posted here a while back about an AI trading bot called Nolly while it was still on testnet.

Small update: it is now live on mainnet, so I went back to check what changed.

The core idea is still the same. You interact with it like a Telegram agent instead of a normal trading UI. You fund the wallet, set rules, pick auto or assist mode, and let it analyze or act based on the instructions you give it.

What feels more complete now:

\- It has clearer commands for wallet, funding, status, logs, and audits

\- You can set trading rules the bot must follow

\- You can switch between autonomous mode and assist mode

\- You can check the last decision, portfolio status, errors, and recent activity

\- You can close a specific position from the bot

\- It also supports natural language shortcuts, which is probably the most interesting part to me

The main thing I’m watching is still risk.

AI trading sounds cool until you remember that bad execution, bad instructions, or bad market conditions can still wreck you. So I’m not judging this like AI will print money. I’m more interested in whether this kind of agent-based trading can make execution cleaner and less stressful for users.

What I like so far is that it does not feel like a random signal bot. It feels more like an agent sitting on top of an on-chain position system, with rules, logs, audits, and manual override.

What I’m still cautious about:

\- How it behaves in high volatility

\- Whether saved instructions actually control behavior well

\- How transparent the decision process stays over time

\- How comfortable users will be with wallet control inside a bot interface

For me, the biggest shift is the interface.

Instead of staring at charts and manually clicking through a dApp, you are basically telling an agent what kind of position you want, how much risk you want, and when to act.

Still early, but I think this is where part of onchain trading is heading.

Curious if anyone else here is testing AI trading agents that actually execute onchain, not just send signals.
